surface_forms: the traffic that exists in Mexico City
description: capital and largest city of Mexico
description_wiki:
Mexico City 
is the capital and largest city of Mexico, as well as the most populous city in North America.
Mexico, officially the United Mexican States, is a country in North America.

aliases: The City of Lights and Flowers
description: consolidated city-borough, and largest city in Alaska, United States of America
description_wiki: Anchorage (Denaʼina: Dgheyay Kaq'; Dgheyaytnu), officially the Municipality of Anchorage, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Alaska.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: American theme park in California owned by The Walt Disney Company
description_wiki: Disneyland is a theme park at the Disneyland Resort in Anaheim, California, United States.

description: United States chain of marine mammal parks, oceanariums, and animal theme parks
description_wiki: SeaWorld is an American theme park chain with headquarters in Orlando, Florida.

description: global theme park chain based on the LEGO brand of toys
description_wiki: Legoland (, trademarked in uppercase as LEGOLAND) is a chain of family amusement parks focusing on the Lego building toy brand.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.
